content.text: Preserve downgrade option is already set to 23.2 (prevents auto-finalize during binary upgrade — good). Let me check the rollout strategy, then update the image.

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb get statefulset crdb-cluster -o jsonpath='{.spec.updateStrategy}' 2>&1; echo; echo "---"; kubectl -n cockroachdb get statefulset crdb-cluster -o jsonpath='{.spec.podManagementPolicy}'; echo

content.text: RollingUpdate strategy. Now I'll upgrade the binaries by updating the statefulset image.

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb set image statefulset/crdb-cluster db=cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.1.0 2>&1

content.text: Now I'll poll the rollout to completion. RollingUpdate updates pods one at a time (highest ordinal first), keeping the cluster available.

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 60); do
  status=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb rollout status statefulset/crdb-cluster --timeout=10s 2>&1)
  echo "$status"
  if echo "$status" | grep -q "successfully rolled out"; then break; fi
  sleep 5
done

content.text: Node 1 keeps showing `is_live=false` despite its `updated_at` advancing and the pod being Ready. Let me investigate more deeply.

input.command:
echo "=== SQL from node0 ==="; kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SELECT node_id, is_live, ranges, leases FROM crdb_internal.kv_node_status JOIN crdb_internal.gossip_liveness USING(node_id);" 2>&1 | head -20
echo "=== underreplicated ranges ==="; kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SELECT sum((metrics->>'ranges.underreplicated')::float) AS under, sum((metrics->>'ranges.unavailable')::float) AS unavail FROM crdb_internal.kv_store_status;" 2>&1 | head

content.text: Node 1 has a high liveness epoch (43 vs 6/4) — it flapped during restart but currently holds a future-dated lease (membership active). 35 ranges are under-replicated, likely rebalancing after the rolling restart. Let me poll until ranges recover.

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 20); do
  u=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SELECT count(*) FROM crdb_internal.ranges_no_leases WHERE array_length(replicas,1) < 3;" 2>/dev/null | tail -1)
  echo "iter $i underreplicated=$u"
  if [ "$u" = "0" ]; then echo "RECOVERED"; break; fi
  sleep 10
done

content.text: Ranges fully recovered (0 under-replicated). Cluster is healthy. Now I'll finalize the upgrade by clearing the preserve_downgrade_option.

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "RESET CLUSTER SETTING cluster.preserve_downgrade_option;" 2>&1

content.text: The upgrade migration runs asynchronously. Let me poll until the cluster version advances to 24.1.

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  v=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SHOW CLUSTER SETTING version;" 2>/dev/null | tail -1)
  echo "iter $i version=$v"
  case "$v" in 24.1*) echo "FINALIZED"; break;; esac
  sleep 6
done

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 40); do
  v=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SHOW CLUSTER SETTING version;" 2>/dev/null | tail -1)
  echo "iter $i version=$v"
  case "$v" in 24.1*) echo "FINALIZED"; break;; esac
  sleep 8
done
